report.title: guinea-pig — structured vulnerability catalog (ground truth)
report.scope: Intentionally-vulnerable fixtures plus their benign counterparts. Positives (`findings` + `secret_catalogue`) are expected to be detected; negatives (`false_positives`) are expected to stay clean. This file is the ground truth consumed by benchmark/run.py.

fields.analysis: static (source code) or dynamic (running service / DAST)
fields.scanner_types: scanner family/families able to detect it: SAST, IAC, PIPELINE, SECRET, DAST, SCA
fields.patched: path to the remediated sibling file (static fixtures only)
fields.false_positives[].kind: remediation (a patched.* file) or lookalike (benign code resembling a rule)
fields.false_positives[].expect: always 'no-finding' — a report here is a benchmark false positive
fields.secret_catalogue: summary of static/CWE-798/catalogue/ — the benchmark expands it per-file

cwe.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input (Classic Buffer Overflow)

findings.title: CWE-120: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
findings.description: Copies an unbounded argv string into a fixed-size stack buffer.

cwe.name: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input (Classic Buffer Overflow)

findings.title: CWE-120: Buffer Copy without Checking Size of Input ('Classic Buffer Overflow')
findings.description: Copies attacker-controlled argv data into a fixed-size stack buffer without bounds checking.

cwe.name: Stack-based Buffer Overflow

findings.title: CWE-121: Stack-based Buffer Overflow
findings.description: Formats attacker-controlled input into a small stack buffer with no size limit.

cwe.name: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes (Prototype Pollution)

findings.title: CWE-1321: Improperly Controlled Modification of Object Prototype Attributes ('Prototype Pollution')

location.snippet: target[key] = merge(target[key], source[key]) without key filtering

findings.description: A recursive merge copies attacker-controlled keys (including __proto__) into a target object.

cwe.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

findings.title: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
findings.description: User-controlled argv is passed directly as the format string.

cwe.name: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String

findings.title: CWE-134: Use of Externally-Controlled Format String
findings.description: Passes attacker-controlled argv directly as the printf format string.

cwe.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound

findings.title: CWE-190: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
findings.description: A multiplication that can wrap around is used as the allocation size.

cwe.name: Integer Overflow or Wraparound

findings.title: CWE-190: Integer Overflow or Wraparound
findings.description: Computes an allocation size by multiplication that can wrap, under-allocating the buffer.

cwe.name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor

findings.title: CWE-200: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
findings.description: S3 bucket ACL is set to public-read, exposing stored objects to anyone on the internet.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

findings.title: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
findings.description: User-controlled filename is combined with a base directory and read without validation.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

findings.title: CWE-22: Path Traversal
findings.description: A user-supplied filename is used to build a filesystem path without validation.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

findings.title: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
findings.description: Untrusted request input is used as a file path without validation, allowing directory traversal.

cwe.name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory (Path Traversal)

findings.title: CWE-22: Path Traversal
findings.description: A file path is built from request input without validation, allowing '../' escapes.

cwe.name: Use of Inherently Dangerous Function

findings.title: CWE-242: Use of Inherently Dangerous Function
findings.description: gets() cannot limit input length and always risks a buffer overflow.

cwe.name: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges

findings.title: CWE-250: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges
findings.description: The workflow grants write-all permissions to the GITHUB_TOKEN instead of least-privilege scopes.

cwe.name: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges

findings.title: CWE-250: Execution with Unnecessary Privileges
findings.description: Pod allows process privilege escalation (allowPrivilegeEscalation: true) and sets no resource limits.

cwe.name: Plaintext Storage of a Password

findings.title: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password
findings.description: A database user password is stored and used in cleartext within the playbook.

cwe.name: Improper Access Control

findings.title: CWE-284: Improper Access Control
findings.description: Pod mounts the host Docker socket, granting container processes root-equivalent control of the host.

cwe.name: Improper Access Control

findings.title: CWE-284: Improper Access Control
findings.description: Security group allows inbound SSH and all traffic from any IP address (0.0.0.0/0).

cwe.name: Improper Certificate Validation

findings.title: CWE-295: Improper Certificate Validation
findings.description: A custom X509TrustManager accepts all certificates, disabling TLS validation.

cwe.name: Improper Certificate Validation

findings.title: CWE-295: Improper Certificate Validation
findings.description: TLS certificate verification is disabled when making an HTTPS request.

cwe.name: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data

findings.title: CWE-311: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
findings.description: EBS volume is created without encryption enabled, storing data at rest in cleartext.

cwe.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

findings.title: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
findings.description: Downloads occur over insecure transport with TLS certificate validation disabled.

cwe.name: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information

findings.title: CWE-319: Cleartext Transmission of Sensitive Information
findings.description: Load balancer listener serves traffic over plaintext HTTP and DB is publicly accessible without encryption.

cwe.name: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key

findings.title: CWE-321: Use of Hard-coded Cryptographic Key
findings.description: A private key committed to the repository (detectable by secret scanners).

cwe.name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
findings.description: Sensitive input is hashed/encrypted using broken algorithms (MD5 and DES).

cwe.name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
findings.description: Weak/broken algorithms (DES, MD5) are used for encryption and hashing.

cwe.name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
findings.description: Passwords are hashed with the broken MD5 algorithm.

cwe.name: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

findings.title: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm
findings.description: Passwords are hashed with MD5, a cryptographically broken algorithm.

cwe.name: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

findings.title: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

location.snippet: new System.Random() used to build a token

findings.description: A security token is generated using the non-cryptographic System.Random PRNG.

cwe.name: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

findings.title: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
findings.description: A security token is generated with a non-cryptographic PRNG.

cwe.name: Use of Insufficiently Random Values

findings.title: CWE-330: Use of Insufficiently Random Values
findings.description: A security token is generated using the non-cryptographic random module.

cwe.name: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)

findings.title: CWE-338: Use of Cryptographically Weak Pseudo-Random Number Generator (PRNG)
findings.description: Math.random() is used to generate a security-sensitive password reset token.

cwe.name: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition

findings.title: CWE-367: Time-of-check Time-of-use (TOCTOU) Race Condition

location.snippet: access() check followed by fopen() write

findings.description: Checks access permission on a path, then opens it for writing, allowing a swap in between.

cwe.name: Insecure Temporary File

findings.title: CWE-377: Insecure Temporary File
findings.description: A temporary file name is generated with mktemp, creating a race condition (TOCTOU).

cwe.name: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime (Memory Leak)

findings.title: CWE-401: Missing Release of Memory after Effective Lifetime
findings.description: Heap memory is allocated but never freed on the return path.

cwe.name: Double Free

findings.title: CWE-415: Double Free

location.snippet: free() called twice on the same pointer

findings.description: The same heap pointer is passed to free() twice.

cwe.name: Double Free

findings.title: CWE-415: Double Free

location.snippet: free() called twice on the same pointer

findings.description: Releases the same heap allocation twice, corrupting the allocator's state.

cwe.name: Use After Free

findings.title: CWE-416: Use After Free
findings.description: A heap pointer is dereferenced after it has been freed.

cwe.name: Use After Free

findings.title: CWE-416: Use After Free
findings.description: Dereferences and writes through a heap pointer after it has been freed.

cwe.name: NULL Pointer Dereference

findings.title: CWE-476: NULL Pointer Dereference
findings.description: The result of malloc() is dereferenced without a NULL check.

cwe.name: NULL Pointer Dereference

findings.title: CWE-476: NULL Pointer Dereference
findings.description: Dereferences a pointer returned by an allocation/lookup that can be null.

cwe.name: Download of Code Without Integrity Check

findings.title: CWE-494: Download of Code Without Integrity Check
findings.description: A remote script is piped directly into a shell with no checksum or signature verification.

cwe.name: Download of Code Without Integrity Check

findings.title: CWE-494: Download of Code Without Integrity Check
findings.description: A remote script is piped directly into a shell with no checksum or signature verification.

cwe.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

findings.title: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
findings.description: An untrusted byte stream is deserialized with BinaryFormatter, allowing arbitrary object construction.

cwe.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

findings.title: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
findings.description: Untrusted bytes are deserialized into a Java object, allowing arbitrary object instantiation.

cwe.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

findings.title: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data
findings.description: Untrusted request body is deserialized with node-serialize, allowing code execution via IIFE payloads.

cwe.name: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

findings.title: CWE-502: Deserialization of Untrusted Data

location.snippet: pickle.loads() and yaml.load() with an unsafe Loader

findings.description: Untrusted bytes are deserialized via pickle and YAML, enabling arbitrary code execution.

cwe.name: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site (Open Redirect)

findings.title: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
findings.description: A user-controlled URL from the query string is used as a redirect target without validation.

cwe.name: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site (Open Redirect)

findings.title: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site ('Open Redirect')
findings.description: Untrusted request input is used as the redirect target without validation.

cwe.name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference (XXE)

findings.title: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference ('XXE')

location.snippet: XmlDocument.LoadXml with XmlResolver set and DtdProcessing = Parse

findings.description: Untrusted XML is parsed with DTD processing and an external resolver enabled.

cwe.name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference (XXE)

findings.title: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference (XXE)
findings.description: Untrusted XML is parsed without disabling external entity resolution.

cwe.name: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference (XXE)

findings.title: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference (XXE)
findings.description: Untrusted XML is parsed with external entity resolution enabled.

cwe.name: Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization

findings.title: CWE-653: Improper Isolation or Compartmentalization
findings.description: Pod shares the host PID, IPC and network namespaces, breaking container/host isolation.

cwe.name: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere

findings.title: CWE-668: Exposure of Resource to Wrong Sphere
findings.description: Pod mounts the host /etc directory via hostPath, exposing host configuration to the container.

cwe.name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

findings.title: CWE-732: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
findings.description: A file is created with world-writable permissions (0777), allowing any user to modify it.

cwe.name: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource

findings.title: CWE-732: Incorrect Permission Assignment for Critical Resource
findings.description: IAM policy grants all actions on all resources (wildcard admin), violating least privilege.

cwe.name: Insufficient Logging

findings.title: CWE-778: Insufficient Logging
findings.description: CloudTrail has log file validation disabled and the S3 bucket has no access logging configured.

findings.title: CWE-787: Out-of-bounds Write

findings.title: CWE-798: Use of Hard-coded Credentials

findings.description: Third-party and official actions are pinned to mutable tags/branches instead of immutable commit SHAs.

findings.title: CWE-829: Inclusion of Functionality from Untrusted Control Sphere
findings.description: The job uses a mutable image tag (node:latest) with no digest, so the pulled code is not pinned.

secret_catalogue.note: One example secret per rule, grouped across scanners (kingfisher, titus, noseyparker, gitleaks, betterleaks). Every entry is a positive the SECRET scanners are expected to flag. See catalogue/README.md.
